qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Storefront account update lets any authenticated user bind and disclose unowned guest addresses
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an authenticated storefront account bug in `PATCH /api/v2/storefront/account` that flows through `Spree::Account::Update` and `Spree::UserAddress#address_not_associated_with_other_user`. The advisory is an unauthenticated guest-checkout bug in the checkout/order path, where plain `bill_address_id` and `ship_address_id` bypass checkout ownership validation and reach `Spree::Order::AddressBook#bill_address_id=` / `ship_address_id=`. The asset class is similar, but the vulnerable entrypoint, model path, and root-cause guard are different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding in this run identifies the advisory's vulnerable checkout root cause. The only real qualifying finding is a separate authenticated storefront account address-binding disclosure, while GHSA-87fh-rc96-6fr6 is the unauthenticated guest checkout `bill_address_id` / `ship_address_id` bypass in the order checkout flow.
evidence: The advisory names `core/lib/spree/permitted_attributes.rb`, `core/app/models/spree/order/checkout.rb`, `core/app/services/spree/checkout/update.rb`, and `core/app/models/spree/order/address_book.rb`, and its PoC targets `/checkout/${ATTACKER_ORDER_TOKEN}/update/address` as a guest user.

The upstream fix commit `15619618e43b367617ec8d2d4aafc5e54fa7b734` changes `Spree::Order::AddressBook#bill_address_id=` and `#ship_address_id=` to require `user_id.present?` and adds checkout-controller coverage for guest users, confirming the advisory root cause is in the checkout/order setter path.

The only qualifying finding folder in `.judge/input_manifest.json` is `storefront-account-unowned-address-disclosure`, whose report and PoC use `PATCH /api/v2/storefront/account`, `Spree::Account::Update`, and `Spree::UserAddress#address_not_associated_with_other_user`, not the advisory's guest checkout path.
